<findings>

[suggestion] [warning] tests/integration/request-dedup.test.ts and tests/unit/viewer/request-dedup.test.ts share the same base filename. Renaming the integration test to messaging-pipeline.integration.test.ts (already recommended on semantic grounds) would simultaneously eliminate this filename collision in test output.

<insights>

- The integration test file named request-dedup.test.ts living in this zone (rather than web-6) suggests the zone boundary was drawn by import proximity, not semantic intent — worth verifying ownership
- As the only messaging primitive with a clear entry point, call-rate-limiter is the natural façade for upstream consumers like web-viewer
- Its imports into both web-5 and web-6 indicate it orchestrates across multiple flow-control primitives, giving it coordinator responsibility
- The integration test 'request-dedup.test.ts' is grouped here by the import graph but semantically tests request deduplication (web-6) — this cross-zone test placement may indicate the rate limiter and dedup utilities are more tightly coupled than their zone split implies.
- The rate limiter zone is absent from the explicit cross-zone import table (web-viewer → web-5/web-6 but not web-viewer → viewer-call-rate-limiter), yet its insights describe it importing into web-5 and web-6. This inversion — the rate limiter pushing into the flow-control and dedup layers rather than being pulled by web-viewer — suggests it sits at the bottom of the messaging dependency stack, not the top as a coordinator role would imply.
- Rate limiter's dependency direction (pushing into web-5/web-6 rather than being consumed by web-viewer) means it is a foundation primitive, not a facade — the 'coordinator' label in existing insights is misleading and should be revised to 'foundation layer'.
- The integration test 'tests/integration/request-dedup.test.ts' is misnamed: it tests the full four-layer pipeline (throttle → coalescer → rateLimiter → dedup) with a createPipeline helper that wires all four utilities. The filename implies dedup-specific ownership but the content qualifies it as a pipeline subsystem test, which explains its ambiguous zone placement by the import graph algorithm.
- tests/integration/request-dedup.test.ts is misnamed and misplaced: it tests the complete messaging pipeline across all four utilities but lives in the rate-limiter zone by import proximity. Renaming it to messaging-pipeline.integration.test.ts and placing it at the messaging subsystem level would align its name, location, and scope.
- The integration test 'tests/integration/request-dedup.test.ts' shares an identical base filename with the unit test 'tests/unit/viewer/request-dedup.test.ts' in the viewer-request-deduplication zone. Test reporters that display filenames without full paths will emit duplicate 'request-dedup.test.ts' entries, making failure attribution ambiguous in CI output.
- tests/integration/request-dedup.test.ts and tests/unit/viewer/request-dedup.test.ts share the same base filename. Renaming the integration test to messaging-pipeline.integration.test.ts (already recommended on semantic grounds) would simultaneously eliminate this filename collision in test output.
- [call graph] 104 internal calls, 9 outgoing, 2 incoming (cohesion: 0.92, coupling: 0.08)

</insights>
